summary: Large cleanup pass to eliminate implicit-function-declaration warnings across fighter character code, especially finishing `ft/chara`. The PR replaced many local `extern` declarations with real header prototypes/includes, tightened internal linkage with `static`/`static inline`, moved shared collision/hurtbox types out of `fighter.h`, added missing baselib/MetroTRK/camera prototypes, and made some matching-sensitive fixes around Zelda reflector attributes and MUST_MATCH pragmas.
what_changed: Removed many file-local `extern` function declarations from fighter character source files and replaced them with proper header includes/prototypes.

Added or expanded headers for functions used across fighter and item code, including `ft_unknown_006.h`, `code_8027CF30.h`, `code_80081B38.h`, `code_8007C114.h`, `code_8007C630.h`, `ftparts.h`, `ftcoll.h`, Samus subheaders, and other character headers.
Converted several helper functions that are only used in one translation unit to `static` or `static inline`, for example Fox special/appeal helpers and Game & Watch aerial action helpers.
Moved the `ftHurt` structure out of `fighter.h` into `lbcollision.h`, with review noting the move was pending a later rename.

Adjusted `jobj.h` dependencies by removing its include of `melee/lb/lbcollision.h`, adding prototypes such as `HSD_JObjDispAll`, and documenting that `HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr` is misplaced because `jobj.h` should not include `lbcollision`.
Added missing prototypes such as `sqrtf__Ff` in `src/MetroTRK/intrinsics.h` and `func_80030E44(s32, Vec3*)` in `src/melee/cm/camera.h`.

Updated Zelda special-N/reflector code around `ftZeldaAttributes::x84`; review discussed changing it from `ReflectDesc*` to `ReflectDesc` and passing `&attributes->x84` to `ftColl_CreateReflectHit` to preserve functional equivalence and matching.
Some Zelda functions were temporarily reverted or gated with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` assembly after correcting the `ftZeldaAttributes::x84` type caused matching complications.
Wrapped a Fox reflector `#pragma dont_inline` workaround with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`, noting the pragma was fake/probably due to inline depth.
Added glossary entries for `chan` as channel and `id` as identifier, not an acronym.
Removed an extra blank line in the Makefile; no functional build-system change is evident from the excerpt.
smart_moves: Centralized declarations in headers instead of suppressing warnings with scattered `extern` declarations, improving type checking across translation units.
Used includes for the subsystem that owns each declaration, such as item creation/removal functions in `code_8027CF30.h` and fighter utility declarations in fighter headers.
Reduced exported symbol surface by marking local-only helpers `static`/`static inline` rather than leaving them as globally visible functions or public header declarations.
Kept matching-sensitive compiler-control pragmas behind `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`, so nonmatching builds are less polluted by hacks while matching builds can preserve codegen.
Identified that Zelda reflector matching was caused by an attribute type/layout issue rather than just an expression syntax issue, preventing a non-functionally-equivalent decomp from being accepted.
Removed `jobj.h`'s dependency on `lbcollision.h`, which is a cleaner dependency direction for baselib headers and avoids broad include coupling.
Explicitly documented known header-placement debt with a `@todo` instead of hiding an uncertain declaration location.
Cleaned an accidental `.orig` file from the tree as part of the warning cleanup.
decomp_lessons: Fixing implicit declaration warnings should usually mean finding or creating the correct owning header, not adding local `extern` declarations in every `.c` file.
When a helper is not shared outside its source file, prefer `static` or `static inline`; this both documents linkage intent and avoids leaking prototypes into character headers.
Header dependency direction matters: low-level baselib headers such as `jobj.h` should not include gameplay collision headers just to make one prototype compile.

Struct field type corrections can affect both code matching and data-section matching; Zelda `ftZeldaAttributes::x84` showed that changing `ReflectDesc*` to `ReflectDesc` also required removing later members from `x88` onward to avoid data-section mismatch.

Pointer arithmetic on raw integers, such as treating `x2D4_specialAttributes` as `u32` and adding `0x84`, is a smell; correct struct typing plus `&attributes->x84` is safer and more decomp-faithful when the layout evidence supports it.

Assembly fallbacks under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` are acceptable temporary bridges when a type/layout fix regresses function matching, but comments should flag that the function can likely be rematched later.
Do not add empty `return;` statements to `void` stubs just for style; review expected clang-tidy `readability-redundant-control-flow` to catch them.
clang-format can support compact empty functions with `AllowShortFunctionsOnASingleLine: Empty`, which helps avoid adding redundant control flow to make empty stubs look intentional.
naming_conventions: `id` is documented as short for `identifier` and explicitly not an acronym.

Character helper names kept the existing subsystem prefix style, e.g. `ftFox_`, `ftGameWatch_`, `ftPikachu_`, `ftZelda_`, and anonymous-address names like `func_800...` where symbols were still unknown.
Comments identify shared character behavior with paired names such as `Fox & Falco's Special Taunt` and `Fox & Falco's Reflector`.
The review notes `ftHurt` was moved to `lbcollision.h` pending a future rename, so the current name/location was not considered final.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` around inline assembly or pragma workarounds so exact-matching builds can preserve codegen without forcing the same hacks into all build modes.
Reverted a few `ftZelda/ftzelda3.c` functions after correcting `ftZeldaAttributes::x84`, with a note that they were probably not hard to rematch.
A Fox reflector helper used `#pragma dont_inline on` as a temporary/fake inline-depth workaround, then guarded the pragma with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` and `#pragma pop`.
A temporary cast in `fighter.c` changed `func_80016C64("PlCo.dat", &pData, ...)` to cast `&pData` to `unk_t`; the author called it a temporary hack because `fighter.c` was next up.

Review feedback emphasized matching and functional equivalence together: `ftColl_CreateReflectHit(fighter_gobj, &attributes->x84, ...)` plus the correct `ReflectDesc` field type was needed rather than only making code compile.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Needed to revert a few functions after correcting `ftZeldaAttributes::x84`; author thought the functions were probably not hard to match later.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The cast to `unk_t` for `func_80016C64` was called a temporary hack because `fighter.c` was next up.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: `ftHurt` was moved to `lbcollision.h`, pending a rename.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Requested removal of empty `return;` statements; clang-tidy `readability-redundant-control-flow` should check this.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Noted clang-format option `AllowShortFunctionsOnASingleLine: Empty` for formatting empty functions compactly.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested `ftColl_CreateReflectHit(fighter_gobj, &attributes->x84, ...)` and changing `ftZeldaAttributes.x84` from `ReflectDesc*` to `ReflectDesc` to fix matching and preserve functional equivalence.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: When the type flip caused a `stage.c` data-section mismatch, advised removing the other members from `x88` onward.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or relocate `ftHurt` after it was moved to `lbcollision.h`.
Find later work that rematches the reverted `ftZelda/ftzelda3.c` functions after the `ftZeldaAttributes::x84` correction.
Find whether `fighter.c` temporary `unk_t` cast around `func_80016C64` was removed in the next fighter cleanup.
Find clang-format or clang-tidy configuration changes adding `AllowShortFunctionsOnASingleLine: Empty` or `readability-redundant-control-flow`.
Audit remaining local `extern` declarations in `ft/chara` after PR 655.
